commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a621e4e83c084429dcf6592184d443471463a29f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram bot-mention metadata to shared inbound context, adds Telegram group prompt guidance tying `@bot` handles to the assistant identity, updates Telegram docs, and adds targeted tests.

PR surface: Source +52, Tests +65, Docs +4. Total +121 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: For the reported Telegram bug, the linked live evidence plus current prompt source make the identity-driven `NO_REPLY` path credible, but I did not run a live Telegram turn on this branch. For the PR defect, yes: the diff makes `requireMention` required and the sole caller omits it, matching the failing type gates.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the external PR still lacks after-fix real Telegram proof and has a type-level blocker; automation should not mark it ready from unit tests alone.

target.title: fix(telegram): bind bot mentions to assistant identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes a Telegram group/topic failure where a turn explicitly addressed to the configured bot handle could run tools and then finalize as `NO_REPLY` because the model treated the handle as someone other than the agent persona.
- Carries explicit bot-mention metadata through the shared inbound context and tells Telegram group prompts that `@<bot username>` is the current assistant identity.
- Keeps `NO_REPLY` available for unrelated group traffic; the change only narrows the ambiguous identity case.
- Out of scope: changing Telegram routing, group admission, allowlists, topic bindings, or delivery-time handling of `NO_REPLY`.

## Linked context

Related to openclaw/openclaw#85654.

Live repro evidence was added at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85654#issuecomment-4703305519.

## Real behavior proof (required external PRs)

- Behavior issue addressed: Telegram forum-topic message explicitly addressed to the bot handle can no longer be presented to the model as if the handle is someone else while still allowing group silence for unrelated traffic.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch; prior live AlphaClaw repro on OpenClaw `2026.6.6` documented in the linked issue comment.
- Exact steps command run after patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- Evidence fix: targeted tests passed across 3 Vitest shards: `src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts`, and `extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`.
- Observed result fix: explicit Telegram bot-handle mentions now set `ExplicitlyMentionedBot=true` and `MentionSource=explicit_bot`, and the Telegram group prompt binds `@openclaw_bot` / `@<bot username>` to the current assistant identity.
- What was not tested: a live Telegram production gateway turn from this branch.
- Proof limitations environment constraints: did not run a second live poller with the production Telegram token; that can steal production updates. The regression is covered at the deterministic context/prompt boundary instead.
- Before evidence: live topic session generated tool work and finalized `assistantTexts:["NO_REPLY"]`; see linked issue comment.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec oxfmt docs/channels/telegram.md extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.session.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.ts src/channels/turn/types.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- `pnpm docs:list`

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, Telegram group/topic prompts now clarify that the configured bot handle is the assistant identity.
- Did config, environment, migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: prompt wording in group chats. Mitigation: provider-gated Telegram-only line, optional structured mention fields, existing `NO_REPLY` policy retained, and tests covering explicit-bot versus non-Telegram behavior.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review and CI.

---
